macro_segments.title: Rami bar Chama's Questions on Worshipped Attached Objects and Status of Ne'evad

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the daf and Rami bar Chama's first question about bowing to grain for minchah
Debate on whether shinuy (change/transformation) affects the status of ne'evad
Proof from fetus of worshipped animal and refutation based on distinction between living growth and manufactured change

macro_segments.title: Reish Lakish's Question on Worshipped Palm Tree and Lulav for Mitzvah

micro_segments.title: Question whether ne'evad mechubar can be used for mitzvah and introduction of bittul and dichui concepts
Proof from covering blood that there is no dichui by mitzvos and uncertainty about whether bittul helps
Unresolved conclusion (teiku) on whether bittul can resuscitate worshipped palm tree for lulav

macro_segments.title: Rav Pappa's Question on Worshipped Animal Wool for Techeiles and Related Instruments

micro_segments.title: Question whether wool from worshipped animal can be used for techeiles, and clarification that this overlaps with prior questions
Rav Pappa's additional questions about using parts of animal for musical instruments of the Levites
Unresolved debate on whether instruments are essential to service or merely enhance it

macro_segments.title: Rava's Question on Worshipped Wellspring Water for Libations

micro_segments.title: Question whether bowing to wellspring affects the water itself or only its source, and status for nisachim
Teaching that publicly owned water cannot become forbidden, and clarification that wellspring flows in private property

macro_segments.title: Mishnah on House Adjacent to Idolatry and Its Reconstruction Rules

micro_segments.title: Mishnah case of house next to idolatry falling down and prohibition on rebuilding it adjacent to idol's property
Solution requiring movement of rebuilt wall four cubits into one's own property and division rules for walls on property line
Gemara's question how non-Jew benefits from gap, and discussion of using space for bathroom, thorns, and other measures

macro_segments.title: Three Types of Houses and Status of Built-for-Idolatry versus Worship-Consecrated

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: house built originally for idolatry forbidden; plastered/prepared for it forbidden only for new additions; idol placed and removed leaves it permitted
Rav's statement that bowing to a house makes it forbidden, treating it unlike trees as never truly mechubar
Gemara resolves that building and worship are treated as one for purposes of bittul, reconciling with mishnah's three categories

macro_segments.title: Three Types of Stones and Status of Platform Stone Made for Idolatry

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: stone hewn for platform (bimus) forbidden; plastered for idolatry forbidden only as addition; idol placed and removed leaves it permitted
Rabbi Ami's statement about plaster needing to penetrate stone itself, challenged by Gemara as overly narrow
Gemara clarifies Rabbi Ami's point is about bittul and that only added material becomes forbidden, not the whole object

topical_tags.term: Idol placed and removed from house
